questions.explanation: 'Let's begin' = Let us begin — suggestion including the speaker.

questions.sentence: Let's not _____ time.
questions.explanation: 'Let's not + base verb' — always use base verb (waste), not wasting/to waste.

questions.sentence: Let's going to the market.
questions.explanation: Let's + base verb. 'Going' is wrong — should be 'go' (base form).

options.english: Let him build a better tomorrow.

options: Let us build a better tomorrow.
questions.answer: Let us build a better tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 'Let us' is the formal version — used in speeches and formal writing.

questions.sentence: Let's _____ the meeting at 10 AM.
questions.explanation: 'Let's start' — always base verb after Let's.

questions.sentence: Let's don't argue about this.
questions.explanation: Correct negative: 'Let's not + base verb'. 'Let's don't' is WRONG.

questions.hindi: चलो समय बर्बाद मत करते हैं।

options.hindi: उसे समय बर्बाद नहीं करने देते।

questions.explanation: Negative suggestion: Let's not + base verb.

questions.sentence: Let _____ consider all the options carefully.
questions.explanation: 'Let us' = formal Let's. 'Let we' is WRONG — always 'Let us'.

questions.sentence: Let's to plan the project together.
options: Let's to plan the project together.
questions.explanation: Never use 'to' after Let's. Let's + base verb (not 'to + verb').
'Let's look at' — correct base verb after Let's.

questions.sentence: Let's _____ our goals for this quarter.
questions.explanation: Base verb 'review' after Let's.

questions.sentence: Let we go to the office now.
options: Let we go to the office now.
Let's go to the office now.
questions.answer: Let's go to the office now.
questions.explanation: 'Let we' is incorrect. Use 'Let's' (Let us).
'Let's start the meeting' — speaker included in the suggestion.

questions.sentence: Let's _____ a plan before we begin.
questions.explanation: 'Let's make a plan' — base verb 'make'.

questions.sentence: Let's not to rush this decision.
options: Let's not to rush this decision.
questions.explanation: 'Let's not rush' — no 'to' after Let's not.

questions.hindi: चलो deadline से पहले काम खत्म करते हैं।

options.hindi: deadline से पहले काम खत्म करो।

options: Let's finish the work before the deadline.

options.english: Let him finish before the deadline.
options.hindi: उसे deadline से पहले खत्म करने दो।

options.english: We will finish before the deadline.
options.hindi: हम deadline से पहले खत्म करेंगे।

questions.answer: Let's finish the work before the deadline.
questions.explanation: 'Let's finish' — suggestion to complete the work on time.

questions.sentence: Let's not _____ any hasty decisions.
questions.explanation: 'Let's not make' — base verb after 'Let's not'.

questions.sentence: Lets celebrate this success.
questions.explanation: 'Let's' always needs an apostrophe. 'Lets' (no apostrophe) means 'allows/permits'.

questions.hindi: चलो टीम को motivate करते हैं।
questions.explanation: Inclusive suggestion — speaker also part of motivating the team.

questions.sentence: Let's _____ transparently with our clients.
questions.explanation: 'Let's communicate' — base verb form.

questions.sentence: Let's going home after the meeting.
options: Let's going home after the meeting.
Let's go home after the meeting.
questions.answer: Let's go home after the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Let's go' not 'Let's going'. Base verb, not gerund.

questions.hindi: चलो अभी कोई decision मत लेते हैं।
options: Let's not make any decision right now.
questions.answer: Let's not make any decision right now.
questions.explanation: 'Let's not make any decision' — negative suggestion including speaker.

questions.sentence: Let us _____ this matter in the next meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Let us discuss' — formal, base verb.

questions.sentence: Let's not to fight about this.
options: Let's not to fight about this.
questions.explanation: No 'to' after 'Let's not'. Correct: Let's not + base verb.

questions.hindi: चलो अपनी गलतियों से सीखते हैं।

options.hindi: वह अपनी गलतियों से सीखता है।

questions.explanation: 'Let's learn from our mistakes' — collective improvement suggestion.

questions.sentence: Let's _____ each other in this difficult time.
questions.explanation: 'Let's support each other' — base verb.

questions.sentence: Let's not missing this opportunity.
questions.explanation: 'Let's not miss' — base verb, not 'missing'.

options.hindi: हम एकता में विश्वास रखते हैं।
उसे एकता में विश्वास रखने दो।

questions.explanation: 'Let us' is the formal version. Used in formal addresses.

questions.sentence: Let's _____ the presentation with a strong conclusion.
questions.explanation: 'Let's end' — base verb.

questions.sentence: Let's celebrates our victory.
questions.explanation: 'Let's celebrate' — no 's' on verb after Let's. Let's + base verb (no third-person 's').
